source.name: local-claude-code
source.readme_claim: 本项目内容可自由复用、参考和学习

mapping.notes: naumi ui starts a Node 20+ terminal frontend and a Python JSONL bridge while preserving legacy fallback; launcher validates the Node runtime before spawning the UI. Client/server event names are pinned in protocol-contract.json and checked by both frontend runtime validation and Python regression tests. terminal-ui now has a process-level fixture that launches the real Python JsonlEngineBridge over stdio and verifies submit, permission confirmation, tool cards, task panel rendering, and resume replay through the Node UI.
Naumi uses a small renderer with explicit footer reservation, ANSI width handling, key tokenization, and bounded viewport rendering.

Python engine events are converted into typed UI messages; terminal components render bounded markdown, code, diff, and tool cards. tool_prepare start/snapshot/end events now drive a live activity card with path, argument size, content size, line count, elapsed time, tool_call_id, and an unknown-total activity meter; prepare_end is retained until the matching following tool_use consumes it, and the final tool card preserves the same prepare progress summary. Mismatched tool_call_id values are not attached to the next tool card. Render cache keys include prepare phase, metrics, and identity so live progress and sequential tools do not reuse stale cards.
The bridge replays persisted engine messages as typed UI messages; the frontend persists local folds and scroll state per session.

default, plan, and bypass modes are exposed through the bridge; Shift+Tab cycles default -> plan -> bypass and the real Python bridge fixture verifies that plan reports permission_mode=strict while bypass reports permission_mode=bypass. Permission requests support y/n/b and Shift+Tab bypass. /permissions renders pending requests, recent permission history, real rule sources, risk levels, confirmation strategy, and bypass scope without mutating state.

Phase one has sticky todo/progress feedback, streamed todo_write previews, persistent footer task activity counts, and a /tasks protocol path for the aggregate task panel. /tasks pin keeps one task panel message refreshed in place when task activity changes and preserves source/status/detail filters. The panel renders real todo owner/blocking metadata, subagent events, permission bubbles, background cwd/pid/ports/output paths, browser run step/current/error details, and a normalized Timeline section built from all visible task sources. /tasks supports read-only source/status filtering such as todo open, background running, and browser needs_input, plus /tasks detail <id> for a read-only detail view. The frontend extracts selectable task ids and supports /tasks select, /tasks next, /tasks prev, /tasks open, /tasks jump, plus empty-input Tab/Enter selection flow. Focused task panels expose a multi-key action bar: Tab/n next, p previous, Enter/o detail, e/c expand-collapse, j records, x cancel, Esc blur; blurred panels restore normal text input. Expanded rows render a lightweight event flow from structured task fields and participate in render-cache keys. Timeline rows now show source counts and support local source folding with /tasks timeline collapse|expand|toggle <source> and /tasks timeline clear; this folding state is included in render-cache keys and has a terminal process test through the JSONL fake bridge. Oversized task panels are bounded by the current body height during full-screen rendering, preserving the top header/summary and an omitted-line notice instead of opening at the panel tail. /tasks jump [id] shows real background output paths or browser artifact/report paths. /tasks cancel [id] sends task_cancel and the bridge dispatches real background_runner.cancel or task_runner.abort_run for background/browser tasks. Full TaskListV2 interactions such as a full-screen list layout remain a later phase.
Frontend and bridge write structured JSONL traces; /debug exposes trace paths and /doctor calls the Python diagnostics backend through the UI protocol.
